target_customer.description: Local families and young adults within 5-mile radius seeking affordable, reliable pizza
target_customer.demographics: Families with children, young adults 18–35, within 5-mile delivery radius
target_customer.psychographics: Value-conscious, convenience-driven, repeat buyers who want consistency and speed
target_customer.primary_pain: Unreliable delivery times and inconsistent quality undermine trust and repeat orders

primary_offer: Pizza delivery and pickup — whole pies, slices, and catering packages

operations.primary_delivery_method: Third-party apps (DoorDash, Uber Eats) + in-house driver

current_goal: Increase profit margin without guessing — identify highest-leverage action and test it first
current_challenges: No item-level margin tracking — unknown which menu items are profitable
No upsell strategy — staff do not suggest add-ons or bundles at checkout
Inconsistent delivery times causing negative reviews and lost repeat orders
Third-party app fees (22–30%) eroding margin on majority of revenue
Food waste estimated at 4–10% of food cost but not measured precisely
constraints_known: Single oven limits throughput during Friday–Sunday peak hours
No direct ordering channel — all delivery through fee-heavy third-party apps

competitors.name: Domino's
competitors.positioning: Speed guarantee, technology-driven ordering, national brand trust

available_data_sources.data_available: Transaction count, revenue by day/hour, top-selling items by volume (not margin)
Order count, average order value, customer ratings, delivery time data
Customer sentiment, recurring complaints, ratings trend

missing_critical_data: Item-level food cost and margin by SKU
Repeat customer rate — no loyalty program or CRM
Direct vs. third-party order split by revenue
Food waste by item measured in dollars
Actual net margin after all costs
